phrasal_verbs.meaning: be persuaded to change your mind (about)
stop making an effort to achieve sth difficult
draw a line through sth written
stay at the same level as
if something dawns on you, you realise it for the first time
do something or deal with something very easily
explain, describe or arrange sth in a clear and detailed way
leave school, etc before you have finished a course

discussion: have a discussion (with sb) about/on sth/doing

D.title: Original key word transformation practice
D.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it keeps the same meaning as the first.

questions.sentence:
Mia began the class project before the lesson on Education and learning. **SET**
The class project ...... before the lesson on Education and learning.

Leo began the study group before the lesson on Education and learning. **OVER**
The study group ...... before the lesson on Education and learning.

Sara began the exam workshop before the lesson on Education and learning. **IN**
The exam workshop ...... before the lesson on Education and learning.

Tom began the online lesson before the lesson on Education and learning. **DEAL**
The online lesson ...... before the lesson on Education and learning.

Nina began the school club before the lesson on Education and learning. **DAWNED**
The school club ...... before the lesson on Education and learning.

Oscar began the revision session before the lesson on Education and learning. **OUT**
The revision session ...... before the lesson on Education and learning.
